title: Grilled Corn and Veggie Kabobs for Summer
meta_description: Discover how to make grilled corn and veggie kabobs perfect for summer gatherings, with tips, recipes, and a handy interlocking corn holder set.
html:
Summer evenings call for fresh, colorful fare that can be tossed on the grill and served straight from the skewer. Grilled corn and veggie kabobs hit that sweet spot—sweet corn, crisp vegetables, and a smoky char that makes every bite memorable. Below you’ll find a step‑by‑step guide for building sturdy, flavor‑packed kabobs, plus a few tricks to keep your corn from slipping off the stick. The Girla Interlocking Corn on the Cob Holders Set is a game‑changer for handling hot corn safely while you prep or serve.

Choosing the Right Ingredients

Start with ingredients that hold up well on the grill. Fresh, in‑season corn on the cob is a must; look for kernels that are plump and milky. For the veg component, pick vegetables that have a similar cooking time to corn—think bell peppers, red onions, zucchini, and cherry tomatoes. Cutting them into uniform pieces (about 1‑inch cubes) ensures everything cooks evenly.

Prep Work: How to Use Interlocking Corn Holders

Before the grill even lights up, set up your corn for easy handling. The Girla Interlocking Corn on the Cob Holders feature silicone handles that snap together, turning a slippery cob into a secure grip. Slip a holder onto each ear of corn, then use the silicone grip to turn the cob while you brush on butter or seasoning. Because they’re dishwasher‑safe, cleanup is a breeze.

Building the Kabobs

Here’s a reliable method for assembling sturdy skewers that won’t fall apart:

  1. Soak wooden skewers in water for at least 30 minutes. This prevents them from burning.
  2. Trim the corn kernels off the cob using a sharp knife, leaving them in long strips. If you prefer whole kernels, carefully slice them away and set them aside.
  3. Thread a piece of corn, then a vegetable, then repeat—alternating colors for visual appeal.
  4. Finish each skewer with a small piece of corn to lock the vegetables in place.

For a quick flavor boost, toss the assembled kabobs in a bowl with olive oil, minced garlic, a splash of lime juice, and a pinch of smoked paprika before grilling.

Grilling Tips for Perfect Char

Preheat your grill to medium‑high (about 400‑450°F). Place the kabobs directly over the heat and close the lid. Cook for 10‑12 minutes, turning every 2‑3 minutes. You’ll know they’re done when the corn is lightly charred and the vegetables are tender but still have a bite.

If you love a smoky crust, finish the kabobs with a quick 30‑second sear over direct flame right before serving. Brush with a little extra butter or a drizzle of your favorite BBQ sauce for a glossy finish.

Serving Ideas and Side Pairings

Serve the kabobs straight from the grill on a platter, letting guests pull off their own pieces. The interlocking corn holders double as serving tools—hand them out for a hands‑free corn experience. Pair the kabobs with a simple cilantro‑lime rice, a fresh corn salad, or a cool cucumber yogurt dip for a balanced summer plate.

Cleaning Up Without the Hassle

After the feast, the Girla Interlocking Corn on the Cob Holders can be tossed straight into the dishwasher. The silicone handles stay bright and non‑slippery, ready for next weekend’s BBQ. Wooden skewers can be discarded or, if you’re eco‑conscious, soak them in warm soapy water and reuse for future grilling sessions.

FAQ

Can I use metal skewers with the corn holders?

Yes, the silicone handles will still grip the corn, but metal skewers conduct heat faster, so watch for quick char on the corn.

Do the interlocking corn holders fit all cob sizes?

They are designed for standard US corn sizes; very large ears may need a slight adjustment, but the silicone grips are flexible enough for most.

Is it safe to grill the corn holders themselves?

The holders are heat‑resistant silicone, but they’re intended for handling corn, not direct grilling. Use them for prep and serving only.
